author: Ahem
text:
Make the integration branch explicit via target_branch

bl review's squash target, bl sync's pushed branch, and the delivery-link / half-push tag scans all read git_current_branch(repo root) independently — an implicit, mutable default a stray git checkout at the root silently re-points, and a hard blocker for the rest of bl-00ca (the forge plugin must know which branch a PR targets).

Add an optional Config.target_branch and resolve every consumer through one seam, Config::integration_branch(root): the configured value wins, else fall back to the root checkout. Mirrors state_remote — unset is byte-identical and never serializes (skip_serializing_if), so old bl ignores it.

Squash routing now keys off bare_squash::squashes_in_place: in-place git merge --squash only when a non-bare root has the integration branch checked out (the default fast path); a bare root, or a target_branch that differs from the checkout, routes through the existing detached worktree so the user's tree is never disturbed. rewind_main mirrors the same predicate so a failed review unwinds the branch it actually moved, and review snapshots the integration branch by name rather than HEAD.

Threaded through review, bare_squash, delivery (now a pure git query taking the resolved branch), sync push, prime ahead-indicator, half-push scan, and claim's worktree base. New unit tests for the knob and squashes_in_place; tests/target_branch.rs covers develop-targeting, the byte-identical default, and sync. Full suite green, clippy clean, 100% coverage.

Known limitation: bl sync does not reconcile concurrent remote movement of a non-checked-out integration branch (it pushes the configured branch but the fetch-merge step still targets the root checkout). Generic non-checkout reconcile belongs with the deferred-squash / forge work in bl-b017; the solo-agent path is unaffected.
